name: Del Met
description_short: Del Met Federal Credit Union is a community-focused credit union established in 1969, serving over 900 members in Muncie, Indiana with $8.8M in assets.
description_long:
Del Met Federal Credit Union was established in 1969 and is headquartered in Muncie, Indiana. As a member-owned financial cooperative, it has grown to serve over 900 loyal members while stewarding approximately $8.8 million in assets. The credit union operates a single branch location at 8000 State Road 3 North and maintains a small but dedicated staff of over 3 employees as of 2026.

Del Met offers a comprehensive range of banking and lending services typical of community credit unions. Members have access to checking accounts (Share Draft accounts with 300+ members), regular savings accounts (900+ members), certificates of deposit (13+ members), credit cards, auto loans for new and used vehicles, personal loans, and mortgage options. The credit union also provides modern digital services including online banking, mobile apps for iOS and Android, e-statements, online bill pay, online loan payments, wire transfers, and financial calculators to help members plan their finances.

Del Met distinguishes itself by catering primarily to multiple educational organizations, positioning itself as a specialized financial institution for the education sector. This niche focus allows the credit union to tailor its products and services to address the specific needs of educators and education-related professionals. The institution maintains active service status monitoring and community engagement through a Q&A forum where members can seek guidance on financial matters.

While Del Met provides solid core banking services, prospective members should note that the credit union operates only one branch with no ATMs, which may limit convenience for those seeking widespread physical access. The lobby is currently closed according to the website, though online and phone services remain available. As a smaller credit union with $8.8M in assets, it may have more limited product offerings and competitive rates compared to larger financial institutions, though member-focused service and educational sector specialization remain key value propositions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Established 1969 — 55+ years of stable, community-focused financial service
Educational sector focus — specialized products and services for educators and education professionals
Comprehensive digital services — online banking, mobile app (iOS/Android), bill pay, e-statements, loan payments
Multiple lending options — auto loans (new/used), personal loans, mortgages, credit cards, CDs
Financial tools — online calculators for auto loans, mortgages, credit card payoff planning
Member-driven Q&A community — peer support and financial guidance forum available
Accessible customer service — phone support at (765) 289-5554, direct branch contact available
cons: Single branch location with no ATM network — severely limited physical access compared to larger banks
Currently closed lobby — in-person services unavailable, forcing online/phone-only interaction
Very small asset base ($8.8M) — may result in limited product offerings and less competitive rates
Niche membership eligibility — primarily serves educational organizations, potentially restricting access for general public
No community reviews available — no member feedback on website to evaluate service quality and member satisfaction
best_for: Educators and education sector professionals seeking specialized financial services
Tech-savvy members comfortable with online/mobile banking who don't need frequent branch visits
Community-minded individuals who prefer member-owned credit unions over large corporate banks
Members seeking personalized service from a small, locally-rooted financial institution
diagnosis: Del Met Federal Credit Union is best suited for educators and education sector employees seeking community-focused, member-owned banking with digital convenience. The primary caveat is the single-branch, no-ATM structure with a currently closed lobby, making it impractical for members requiring frequent physical branch access or immediate in-person service.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
